about: Starter library of ready-to-enable architectural invariants: the 5 structural tenets from https://blog.k10s.dev/im-going-back-to-writing-code-by-hand/ (T1–T5) PLUS 2 layering/test-rot invariants (L1–L2) learned from adopting this gate on real repos — a class of decay the size/coupling metrics are blind to. Copy the entries you want into your project's invariants.json and TUNE the thresholds/symbols/paths to your codebase. 'status: ready' = implemented check kind; 'status: planned' = on the roadmap (design §4), template shows the intended shape.

tenets.tenet: T1 — Architecture is yours; the coordinator stays thin
tenets.intent: The central struct/class stays a thin coordinator; per-view/feature state lives in its own type — not one God Object (the k10s Model: 30+ fields, 110-case Update).
tenets.tune: Set `struct` to your coordinator type; `max` to a sane ceiling; keep `ratchet` so it can't grow silently.

tenets.tenet: T2 — Per-view isolation; no central dispatch growth
tenets.intent: No ever-growing central branch on a view/mode identity (the k10s `if currentGVR == ...` / 110-case switch). New views plug in via an interface, not new cases.
tenets.tune: Point `on` at the dispatch discriminant. Ratchet-only (a fixed `max` of ~3 also works).

tenets.tenet: T4 — Typed records; never positional arrays
tenets.intent: Data is typed structs/records, never flattened to arrays accessed by magic integer indices (the k10s `ra[3]` = Alloc fragility that breaks silently when a column moves).
tenets.tune: max 0 is strict; raise + ratchet if a legacy area has unavoidable positional access (or use `// anchor:allow no-positional-rows: <reason>`).

tenets.tenet: T5 — Mutations on the main loop; workers send messages
tenets.intent: Background workers must NOT mutate shared state directly (the k10s data race: goroutines writing Model fields with no mutex). They send messages; the main loop mutates.
engines: ast-grep (TS): a property assignment inside a timer/async callback
tenets.tune: Set `inside` to your async entrypoints (setInterval/setTimeout/queueMicrotask/worker.onmessage); `pattern` to the forbidden mutation. Go is supported via @ast-grep/lang-go using inside_kind: go_statement (the heuristic engine cannot express this).

tenets.tenet: T3 — Explicit scope boundary; fight scope creep
tenets.intent: Stay within the project's declared scope (the k10s drift from a GPU dashboard into a k9s clone). New top-level capabilities require a deliberate scope change.
engines: engine-agnostic: extracts dispatch case-labels, flags any not in allowed
tenets.tune: Set `allowed` to in-scope capability names (flags out-of-scope renames a ratchet misses) and/or `allowedDirs` to in-scope top-level feature dirs (flags a whole new feature area appearing).

tenets.tenet: L1 — Layering: product must not depend on process/working-state
tenets.intent: Product code (src/, test/) must not reach into a lower/sibling layer's working state — e.g. tests reading the build/orchestration scratch dir. Wrong-direction dependencies make the product un-extractable and couple it to ephemeral state (the failure that fused a real repo's test suite to its pipeline's coordination/ tree). Size/coupling metrics never see this.
engines: language-agnostic: counts files under `dirs` that reference `path` in CODE (comment citations are stripped first, so provenance notes don't trip it)
tenets.tune: PER-PROJECT — only forbid a path that is genuinely a DIFFERENT layer your product shouldn't touch. Set `path` to that layer's dir (regex); `dirs` to your product layers. Do NOT forbid a path your product legitimately owns (e.g. on the orchestrator repo itself, coordination/ IS the product domain). Suppress legacy refs with `// anchor:allow product-not-read-process: <reason>`.

tenets.tenet: L2 — No time-bomb tests (tests that can only rot)
tenets.intent: No test pinned to a FROZEN git reference — `git diff <ref>..HEAD`, `--name-only` anti-scope diffs, `git show <sha>`, or a pinned-SHA const. Such a test can only rot: once HEAD moves past that point it fails regardless of product correctness. Round/PR-scoped checks (anti-scope, frozen-surface) belong in a round-aware GATE (see ../install-hook.sh / a pre-merge script), not the permanent suite.
engines: language-agnostic: flags test files (*.test.* / *.spec.*) that BOTH invoke git AND carry a frozen-ref marker (so a product SHA-256 hash test that never touches git is not flagged)
tenets.tune: Universal (no product should pin a frozen ref) — usually adopt as-is. Set `dirs` to your test roots. Suppress a deliberate, maintained case with `// anchor:allow no-time-bomb-tests: <reason>`.
